operator vision 2026-06-17: intelligent + methodical compaction running in parallel to the pipeline, extracting wheat from textual chaff for either long-term persistence or runtime overhead reduction

the DREAM MECHANISM = the project's name for the periodic idle-time memory-hygiene job (the off-path ConsolidationPipeline, internal/agent/memory/consolidation_phases.go, already runs phaseWithinTierDedup); its value is chiefly the PERSISTENCE layer — don't redundantly store a tokenized 'fact' that is really an instance of an existing learning; REINFORCE the existing learning (strengthen/merge) instead of duplicating it (associative reinforcement, richer than exact dedup; supersession per prefer-latest-value)

two-speed consolidation: fast per-turn distillation (lossy, fact-floored) feeds the slow dream pass (LOSSLESS IN RELEVANT CONTENT — preserves every learning, drops only redundant/irrelevant storage; 'lossless' scoped to relevance, not bytes) — separates the two reduction kinds so each has the right safety bar

items.title: Shape complete introspection summaries — the model should reliably cover all aspects of its subagent functionality, not a subset
items.summary: Operator-aligned shaping item 2026-06-17 (don't excuse model variance — shape it, per feedback_no_severity_tier_excuses + the preemptive-shaping thesis). RCA (v1.2.0 release-gate soak, recorded state): introspection_discovery ('use your introspection tool to discover your current subagent functionality and summarize it') intermittently produced a summary covering only 1 of 3 expected aspects (check_introspection_summary: '1/3 evidence markers'), then passed 3/3 on re-run with fuller summaries. This is MODEL VARIANCE in summary completeness, NOT a framework defect (the model produced a substantive answer; nothing degraded it; verifier_retry_suppressed) — so it cleared the release gate via the stability criterion. But the standing thesis is to SHAPE the model to produce a complete summary at generation time rather than rely on it varying into completeness: the introspection-summary surface should structure the model toward covering each aspect the introspection tool returns (creation/management, status, roles, etc.) — e.g. a structured introspection result the model is shaped to enumerate, or a completeness hint in the summary instruction, so coverage is reliable rather than a coin-flip. attribution_only / low gating impact, but in scope because variance is a shaping opportunity, not an excuse. Scope: confirm the introspection tool returns all aspects (so it is a SUMMARY-completeness issue, not a tool-data issue), then shape the summary toward full coverage; validate the completeness across repeated runs.
evidence: RCA (recorded state): introspection_discovery produced 1/3 evidence markers once, 3/3 on re-run — model summary-completeness variance, not a framework defect (substantive answer, no swap, verifier_retry_suppressed)

items.title: Collective release-soak orchestration harness — one command runs, serializes, routes, gates, and scorecards the whole behavioral suite
items.summary: Operator observation 2026-06-17: the 6 release soak runners (run-agent-behavior-soak.py, run-adversarial-soak.py, run-memory-grounding-soak.py, run-prompt-compression-soak.py, run-embedder-soak.sh, run-soak-fuzz.sh) can be run INDIVIDUALLY, which is great, but there is no COLLECTIVE harness to run them as one suite — so the full release gate is currently hand-choreographed by the release driver each cycle (this v1.2.0 ceremony is the live proof: all six were sequenced by hand, each with bespoke per-runner env, serialized on the shared :8080, and pass counts hand-aggregated into the precondition-10 scorecard). Build a single orchestrator (e.g. scripts/run-release-soak-suite.sh + a `just release-soak` recipe) that encodes, in CODE rather than as tribal knowledge in release-procedure.md, the whole suite: (1) RUN ORDER + SERIALIZATION — runners share the managed server port, so the harness sequences them (or parameterizes ports) and never runs two managed servers at once; (2) PER-RUNNER ROUTE POLICY — behavior-battery + adversarial + memory-grounding + prompt-compression on the cloud provider release route (SOAK_ALLOW_NONLOCAL_MODEL=1 + cloud-provider/cloud-provider-v4-pro), the embedder soak against the real bundled sidecar, weak-model-floor calibration on local-model-b, fuzz model-independent — currently documented prose, should be the harness's own table; (3) GATE-ON-RESOLUTION — the suite fails if ANY release_gate scenario fails and surfaces attribution_only/stress_only findings DISTINCTLY without auto-passing on the label (feedback_no_severity_tier_excuses); (4) ONE AGGREGATE SCORECARD — emit the single release scorecard the release notes / Release Truth / soak-suite-audit consume, so it is a build output, not hand-copied (feedback_quantitative_and_qualitative: keep machine pass counts and qualitative grades distinct); (5) PROVENANCE — stamp git commit + dirty state and refuse-or-loudly-flag a dirty tree for release evidence (the v1.2.0 ceremony had to re-run the battery on a clean commit precisely because the prior 38/38 ran git_dirty:True); (6) PRODUCTION SAFETY — never target :8080 / ~/.flowforge-dev, always a ~/.flowforge-dev clone/fresh (feedback_protect_production_instance); (7) COST AWARENESS — reuse CI/soak evidence on tree-identical commits and scope re-runs to what actually changed (per-turn-retry-cost-ceiling + feedback_release_notes_artifact_and_ci_reuse). Net: the release ceremony invokes one gated command instead of orchestrating six by hand, and the scorecard artifact is generated rather than transcribed.
evidence: no collective orchestrator exists today: justfile has only `soak-fuzz` (fuzz runner alone) and `ci-test` (which chains soak-fuzz, not the behavioral suite); the 6 release runners are invoked one-by-one with bespoke env

the per-runner model-route policy (the cloud provider for 4, real embedder + local-model-b floor, model-independent fuzz) lives as prose in docs/testing/release-procedure.md - encode it in the harness so it cannot drift from the docs

items.title: Per-use-case model recommendations (up to 3 local + 3 cloud) from benchmark quant/qual/latency

items.title: Recognize the operator's own channel as operator/creator authority (not Peer)
items.summary: BUG (operator-reported 2026-06-17, counter-to-intent, hobbles the agent): over Chat Connector the agent treats its chat with the operator as PEER-level, not operator/creator. RCA from recorded state (production flowforge.toml): [security] allowlist_authority='Peer', trusted_authority='Creator', trusted_sender_ids=[] (empty), and [channels.chat-connector] allowed_chat_ids=[] — so the operator's own sender is in NEITHER trust layer and the session falls to the default Peer authority (session.Authority -> policy engine gates authority-bound tools). TrustedSenderIDs grants Creator but is unset. Two layers: (a) config gap — the operator's sender id is not trusted; (b) DESIGN gap (the counter-to-intent part) — the system requires manual trusted_sender_ids wiring and has no automatic 'the operator's own configured channel is operator-level' recognition. Systemic fix: recognize the operator's configured channel/identity as operator(or creator)-level by design (ties to multi-user-rbac-operator-delegated-roles), not a manual footgun. Behavioral-correctness now-item (paramount).

items.title: False capability-denial ships: HARD guard's corrective retry suppressed + turn-policy widening valve never fires
items.summary: BEHAVIORAL-CORRECTNESS (answer-fidelity / false-incapacity class; operator-reported 2026-06-18, code-confirmed RCA). SYMPTOM: on Chat Connector, 'clean up the workspace' got a FALSE refusal with a fabricated permanence/authority narrative ('blocked by tool surface restrictions ... only four tools ... peer trust ... authority policy stops me ... log into the operator console'); the very next turn exposed a 15-tool surface and the agent self-corrected ('I was wrong'). RECORDED STATE (state.db session 8faa17de, denial turn 97f03864, 2026-06-18 05:07): denial turn request_tools=4/degraded, next turn request_tools=15/ok — the 'four tools' count was literally true FOR THAT TURN; the PERMANENCE + AUTHORITY framing was fabricated. Events: seq7 tool_call_finished success (successful_tools=1); seq8 tool_call_blocked reason_code=tool_not_selected tool=list-open-tasks; seq15 guard_contract_evaluated internal_jargon severity=hard violation='semantic NARRATED_DELEGATION margin exceeded' recovery=retry; seq16 guard_retry_suppressed (reason: 'turn already made substantive execution progress and the guard finding is narrative-only', successful_tools=1); seq17 response_finalized (false denial shipped). CAUSAL CHAIN (3 defects, A+C chained): DEFECT C (upstream trigger, turn_policy.go:130 DeriveTurnEnvelopePolicyClassified) routed the short casually-phrased request to the LightweightToolSurface branch (turn_policy.go:186-196: simple/conversational/words<=24) -> 4-tool surface -> list-open-tasks blocked out-of-surface (the genuine 'first pass insufficient' trigger). The design's built-in recovery is Expanded() (turn_policy.go:212-223, AllowRetryExpansion=true) — widen the light envelope once proven insufficient. DEFECT A (PRIMARY, the ship decision; post_success_retry_policy.go + guard_retry.go + guards_truthfulness_execution.go) SUPPRESSED that corrective HARD-guard retry because successful_tools=1 and the finding was classed 'narrative-only' — conflating 'a tool ran' with 'final answer acceptable', and 'narrating internal mechanics' (style) with the narration BEING a false capability/authority verdict (correctness). So C under-provisions, the valve would fix it, A jams the valve shut -> starved surface persists -> false permanent refusal. DEFECT B (detection gap; guards_truthfulness_capability.go:22-68) the dedicated capability-denial guard matches a hardcoded lexical phrase allowlist ('i cannot execute' etc.) that the novel phrasing matched NONE of; the semantic NARRATED_DELEGATION signal DID fire and should drive capability grounding instead of the brittle list. ACCEPTANCE: (1) RED-first repro test: turn with successful_tools>=1 + HARD NARRATED_DELEGATION/capability-denial contract firing + final content denying a capability the FULL surface supports -> currently ships denial; after fix must RETRY+widen not suppress. (2) A: suppression-after-progress gated on violation CLASS — HARD contracts that are capability/authority/delegation falsehoods are NEVER suppressed by execution-progress. (3) B: capability/authority denials grounded against the ACTUAL+FULL tool surface + current trust/authority scope before finalize, driven by the semantic signal not the lexical list; a denial contradicted by the real surface is rejected/retried; a per-turn lightweight surface must NEVER be reported as a PERMANENT capability boundary (ties operator-authority-recognition/A1). (4) C: a tool_not_selected block on a tool the task needs triggers Expanded()/escalation not a narrated refusal; and/or task-vs-conversational synthesis must not down-classify a substantive multi-step request purely for being short. (5) Rule-14: record WHY retried-vs-suppressed (contract class + grounding verdict + whether envelope widened). OUT OF SCOPE (do not regress): the milder sibling — agent does real verified cleanup then says 'I paused for your call before deleting AMBIGUOUS files' — is ACCEPTABLE (verified-true self-report); the fix targets FALSE capability verdicts contradicted by the real surface, NOT honest narrated pauses. SOAK-GATED: hot-path guard/turn-policy behavioral change; land with soak validation (the gate-on-resolution discipline), sequences with the behavioral-correctness cluster (#2 tool-surface, answer-fidelity inverse-soak).

items.title: SelfHealing = framework-native deterministic maintenance subsystem (NOT subagent delegation)
items.summary: ARCHITECTURE (operator 2026-06-18, SUPERSEDES the 'self-healing orchestrates via subagent delegation' framing). THESIS: fundamental maintenance must be framework-native deterministic the platform, not LLM-driven/authority-gated/refusable delegation. Model: internal/db/hygiene.go RunHygiene on the maintenance heartbeat — generalize to every domain. WHY NOT DELEGATION (explicit non-goal): authority-gated (blocked at peer trust — the false-incapacity authority over-generalization), non-deterministic (can refuse/confabulate/stall), costly (inference on rule-based work), demonstrably failed (agent spawned 125 dead 'workspace-cleanup' pending tasks trying to delegate ONE cleanup it couldn't authorize). The self-healing NEVER routes fundamental upkeep through delegation. ARCH: one subsystem, deterministic per-domain repairers (pure the platform, idempotent, typed sub-report) aggregated into one MaintenanceReport (extends HygieneReport); runs on the maintenance loop AND on-demand (full/single-domain) + report/dry-run mode; runs with SYSTEM authority BY CONSTRUCTION (framework code, not a user-channel action) — DECOUPLES the self-healing from the subagent-spawn-authority defect (that fix is still needed for user-facing delegation but is NO LONGER a prerequisite for maintenance). JUDGMENT ESCALATION (bounded/rare): most maintenance is rule-based + needs no LLM; ONLY genuinely-ambiguous cases (is this file a user's working file or detritus?) may make a NARROW grounding-judge-style call that PROPOSES/quarantines only, never auto-destructive, never a subagent. SAFETY: risk-tiered defaults (low-risk DB/index may auto-apply; WORKSPACE/CONFIG/PLUGINS/SKILLS/SECURITY default report-only/propose until enabled); destructive=quarantine-first/reversible (extend mechanic_quarantine; files move-to-quarantine; DB backup-then-mutate + integrity check; single-writer/quiesced — state.db corrupted once under contention); Rule-14 what/why/reversal; report distinguishes auto-applied vs proposed. ACCEPTANCE: single framework entry point runs all enabled deterministic domain repairers -> one MaintenanceReport (DB+workspace+config+plugins+skills+security), NO dependency on subagent spawning or LLM for rule-based passes; each repairer RED-first + typed counters + quarantine/reversal + report-only default for sensitive domains; any LLM judgment is bounded/propose-only/off the rule-decidable critical path.

items.title: Instruction skills return raw body AS the response (skill-first), bypassing the model — answer-fidelity defect
items.summary: BEHAVIORAL-CORRECTNESS / answer-fidelity (operator-reported 2026-06-18; PROVEN from code + instance, read-only). SYMPTOM: in the operator's Chat Connector conversation, a ghola CLI usage block ('Use ghola as the default CLI for URL/HTTP requests...') is emitted as the assistant reply on nearly every turn, replacing the real answer (e.g. asked to 'fetch up to date headlines on Iran', it dumped the ghola how-to instead of fetching). ROOT CAUSE (proven): skillAdapter.TryMatch (internal/daemon/daemon_adapters_execution.go:217-224) returns an Instruction skill's raw markdown Body DIRECTLY as the Outcome.Content (the assistant response), and trySkillFirst (src/engine/pipeline_stages_synthesis.go:72) runs this BEFORE inference for Operator-authority turns. So any trigger-keyword match short-circuits the model and emits the canned skill text. The installed skill ~/.flowforge-dev (enabled=1) has absurdly broad trigger keywords [ghola, url, REST, http, api, download, fetch, request, endpoint] — all common words — so it fires constantly. This DIRECTLY violates the design north-star (the framework must PRESERVE the model's cognition, never replace a correct answer with canned text) and feedback_canned_responses_panic_only. CORRECTION: an earlier session WRONGLY attributed this to a Provider B model Code environment injection by mistaking the operator's pasted EVIDENCE in Provider B model Code transcripts for a live injection; it is a FlowForge defect. FIX DIRECTION (proposed): Instruction skills must STEER the model (inject the body as guidance into the model's context) rather than be returned verbatim as the response; never short-circuit inference with a skill body. Separately, keyword activation is substring-loose and should require relevance, not bare common-word matches. RULE 17: proven from the actual source + the enabled skill file, not plausibility.
evidence: internal/daemon/daemon_adapters_execution.go:217-224 (Instruction skill Body returned as Outcome.Content)
src/engine/pipeline_stages_synthesis.go:72 (trySkillFirst before inference, Operator authority)

items.title: Proactive episodic recall — memory surfaces relevant past events unprompted (life-lieutenant shoulder-tap)
items.summary: APPROVED for v1.3.0 (operator 2026-06-18; relayed from agent persona Duncan). Today recall is REACTIVE (memory_recall on query). Ask: surface relevant past events on the agent's own initiative (e.g. 'you schedule international calls during your dinner window'; 'a contact you haven't messaged in 8 months has a birthday next week'). Fits the life-lieutenant vision + memory-as-a-brain north-star; the introspection is grounded (the layered memory episodic/semantic/working/knowledge_facts/hippocampus exists; recall is reactive-only). ARCHITECTURAL TENSION (surfaced by the dev session, must gate): proactive recall IS unsolicited injection of content into turns — the exact surface behind the two answer-fidelity defects just fixed (Symptom A monolith history-bleed; Symptom B instruction-skill body injected every turn) and the agency-churn history (125 dead delegated tasks, 23k audit rows, runaway loops from one junk question). Building proactive injection on a path just proven leaky, in a cost-sensitive runtime, is the risk. DEPENDENCY-GATED ON: (1) injection-surface defects RESOLVED + soak-proven (Symptom A scoping/session-hygiene; instruction-skill steer+semantic validated); (2) a hard RELEVANCE + TIMING gate (reuse the semantic-relevance + grounding-judge discipline — only high-signal, time-pertinent items, never noise); (3) an ANSWER-FIDELITY guard: surface as a DISCRETE shoulder-tap, never derail/replace the model's response to the user's actual turn; (4) COST bounds (runs work every turn; operator cost-sensitive); (5) frequency/dedup so it does not nag (agency-churn lesson). Evaluated on merits + safeguards, not on the 'demo of initiative' framing; grant/sequence is the operator's product call.
evidence: relayed from FlowForge agent persona 'Duncan' (operator 2026-06-18)
gated on: flowforge-monolith-session-history-bleed + instruction-skill-body-returned-as-response (injection-surface resolution)
agency-churn history: internal/db/hygiene_agency.go repairAgencyChurnArtifacts
reactive recall today: memory_recall tool path

IDLE-TIME CLOSURE pitch (Duncan via operator 2026-06-18): extend to a cron/idle-time wake-up that reviews memory and surfaces unclosed loops + Obsidian vault cross-referencing (/home/user/artifact Vault). GROUNDED CORRECTION (read-only prod state.db): working_memory=4763 rows (NOT the claimed 289); 2829 are unresolved_question = the AGENCY-CHURN accretion class (the 23k-audit-row runaway repairAgencyChurnArtifacts prunes), so row-count != actionable loose-ends. episodic=10334 but carries 391 financial_event (partly misclassified) + basalt-orchid test contamination. SAFETY SPLIT (the key design contract): the VALUABLE+SAFE core = a FILTERED, ranked list of high-signal unclosed loops surfaced as a PROPOSE-only read-only deliverable for the operator; the RISKY part = idle-time AUTONOMOUS closure (cron wake->review->ACT), which re-arms the exact agency-churn mechanism and must be gated hardest. Vault WRITE/auto-edit of personal notes is propose-only too. Depends on the hygiene/relevance filtering (prune the churn pile) + the injection-surface fixes landing first, else any scan returns the flood not a clean list.

CORRECTED DESIGN (operator 2026-06-18; grounded in code): do NOT invent a propose-vs-act gate — the agency framework already governs degree-of-self-closure. Proactive recall/closure = EXTEND the existing agency MemoryProvider (internal/agency/memory_provider.go, the CandidateProvider port in service.go) to emit richer unclosed-loop / pattern / vault-cross-ref CandidateActions; a vault provider follows the same port. Execution degree is ALREADY governed by: AgencyLevel (disabled..aggressive = discovery aggressiveness), AutonomyLevel (always_ask..never_ask = ask-vs-act; default always_ask), AllowedActionClasses + IsReadOnlyActionClass (action_class.go read-only confinement), AllowedScopes (must include memory/vault), MaxCandidateAttempts (anti-churn parking). Vault writes classify as a write action-class -> gated by ask_for_writes. The propose-only first step is simply AutonomyLevel=always_ask, NOT a new mechanism. The REAL engineering work is clean, RANKED candidate generation (relevance + hygiene so the 2829-row unresolved_question churn does not swamp candidates), wired through the existing provider->policy->pipeline path.

items.title: Agency/autonomy governance hardening — read-only confinement fails open + anti-churn budget ignores failures (+4)
items.summary: BEHAVIORAL-CORRECTNESS / safety (audit 2026-06-18, code-verified). The agency/autonomy skeleton is sound (fail-closed defaults Enabled=false/AgencyLevel=disabled/AutonomyLevel=always_ask; double-gated scope; evidence-first; real authority-confirmation gate ws_control_config.go; defense-in-depth on execute disposition), but the audit found live defects that self-closure would lean on: D1 (CRITICAL) read-only confinement FAILS OPEN — filterReadOnlyToolDefs/blockMutatingToolUnderReadOnly drop a tool only when MakesExecutionProgress is true, which is false for OperationUnknown (semantics.go default), and EVERY unenumerated MCP tool (Slack send_message, GitHub create_pull_request, fs create_file, ...) is OperationUnknown -> a read-only-class agency action (summarize/diagnose) can load such a tool via request_tools and mutate external state; FIX: read-only paths must gate on !IsReadOnlyExploration (allowlist, fails closed), not MakesExecutionProgress. D2 (HIGH) anti-churn parking ignores failures — ExecutedAttemptCount counts only outcome='executed' (agency_repo.go:208) but a failing tick writes execution_failed (daemon_agency.go:210), so a deterministically-failing candidate never reaches MaxCandidateAttempts and re-executes+re-bills every cadence forever (re-arm of the v1.1.1 runaway on the failure branch); FIX: count execution_failed toward the budget (keep consistent with hygiene_agency.go:59). D3 (HIGH) candidate risk flags (RequiresWrite/ExternalEffect/Destructive/CredentialSensitive/Financial/PrivacySensitive) are dropped reconstructing the candidate from the decision (agencyCandidateFromDecision) -> executor cannot re-gate sensitive actions; persist them. D4 (MED) queue_scheduler_job can reach execute_allowed with no executor -> unbounded full-surface model turn; implement a narrow executor or don't let executor-less classes execute. D5 (MED) AllowedScopes/AllowedActionClasses accept arbitrary unvalidated strings (validateAgency) -> silent misconfig; validate membership. D6 (MED, Rule 14) decision audit omits risk basis + effective autonomy/agency level -> can't RCA an autonomous action; record them. H1 (quality) ProactiveMemory surfaces unresolved_question by raw importance with no relevance/recency floor (2829 prod rows) -> noise/nag; add relevance ranking + per-cycle cap. SEQUENCE: D1+D2 first (safety invariants self-closure leans on), then D3+H1, then D4/D5/D6. Gates the proactive-episodic-recall feature.

items.title: security.threat_caution_ceiling has 3 contradictory schemas -> config-save deadlock (bricks installs); + self-heal repair
items.summary: BEHAVIORAL-CORRECTNESS / security-config (operator-reported 2026-06-18 Windows install, code-verified). ThreatCautionCeiling is an AuthorityLevel (security_claim.go:107 default AuthorityExternal; names External/Peer/SelfGenerated/Operator per types.go:201). But validation is INCOHERENT across THREE schemas: core/config_validation.go validateSecurityCeiling accepts {Safe,Caution,Dangerous,External,Creator} (stale Creator not Operator; not the authority set); configstate/schema.ts knownEnums (drives the UI/API config schema) declares {low,medium,high,critical}; neither matches the AuthorityLevel type. RESULT (deadlock): the UI offers low/medium/high/critical, the operator picks 'medium', it persists, and core validation rejects it forever -> EVERY config save fails validation -> primary model can't persist, a removed model reappears, security options can't be changed (the operator's Windows install: 'TOML borked around security options such that I cannot make changes'). FIX: single-source the ceiling enum to the AuthorityLevel names {External,Peer,SelfGenerated,Operator} (+ 'Creator' backward-compat alias, matching the other authority fields) shared by BOTH core validation AND configstate so the UI stops offering invalid values; complete the Creator->Operator rename here. PLUS a config repair/migration that heals existing TOMLs with an invalid ceiling value (medium/low/high/critical/Creator) -> a valid value (External default; Creator->Operator) on load AND via the self-healing config-repairer, so installs self-heal WITHOUT hand-editing TOML (operator: 'the self-healing should detect and repair'; no production surgery). Likely the root of the model-persistence symptoms (verify those resolve once saves validate again).

src/configstate/schema.ts:53 (knownEnums {low,medium,high,critical})

items.title: Embedded Runtime tool_calls arguments serialized as object but Provider A-compat endpoint wants string -> 400 breaks local tool use
items.summary: BEHAVIORAL-CORRECTNESS / provider-compat (operator-reported 2026-06-18 Windows, code-verified). embeddedRuntimeAdapter.Qualifies(FormatEmbedded) (internal/llm/tool_message_normalization_providers.go:78) serializes assistant tool_calls via embeddedRuntimeAdapter (:189) -> embeddedRuntimeAdapter, emitting function.arguments as a JSON OBJECT (embedded-runtime NATIVE format). But FormatEmbedded is sent to the Provider A-COMPATIBLE endpoint (internal/llm/client_url.go:29 'FormatProviderA, FormatEmbedded, and all others use Provider A-compatible endpoint'), whose schema expects arguments as a STRING. Result: embedded-runtime returns 400 'json: cannot unmarshal object into the platform struct field .messages.tool_calls.function.arguments of type string' on any multi-turn turn that replays a prior tool_call -> breaks tool use for ALL embedded-runtime tool-calling models (local-model-b, local-model-a, etc.) through the framework. Same incoherency class as the config-ceiling triple-schema: two parts of the code disagree on a contract (the message normalizer's wire format vs the endpoint's). Surfaced exercising embedded-runtime/local-model-b DELEGATION:C1. FIX: align FormatEmbedded outbound tool_calls to the endpoint actually used — string arguments (Provider A format, like providerACompatibleToolCalls); if the embedded-runtime-native object-args path is dead (no native endpoint is used), remove it (don't keep a serializer that contradicts the endpoint). RED-first: a FormatEmbedded request replaying a prior tool_call serializes arguments as a STRING.
evidence: internal/llm/tool_message_normalization_providers.go:78,138,189 (embeddedRuntimeAdapter object args)
internal/llm/tool_message_normalization_providers.go:173 (providerACompatibleToolCalls string args)
